title: PR-2 sim-core owner version and caller-owned output surfaces
summary: Add version-bound and caller-owned allocation-free public surfaces for MapGrid-assisted GridPathfinder, WorkOfferIndex, ReservationLedger and M3AbilityCacheStore so WM-0170 can resume unchanged.
acceptance: WorkOfferIndex adds typed ownerVersion, per-row version and monotonic indexVersion lanes. Successful version-bound register/update/remove changes the exact versions once, failed mutation changes none, and owner/index versions remain bound to the selected row rather than supplied as unrelated caller metadata

WorkOfferIndex exposes caller-owned registerOfferInto, updateOfferInto, readOfferInto and selectTopOffersInto (or equivalently reviewed names) whose reusable mutation/read/selection outputs include offer id, owner version, row version, index version, target, score and existing cap metrics without per-call result/array allocation

GridPathfinder exposes findPathInto using a caller-owned bounded Uint32Array route lane plus reusable result output for success and every structured failure; it preserves request/basis/start/goal/cost/node-expansion facts, reports output-capacity failure without partial route publication and allocates no result path

MapGrid additively exposes readMovementCellByIndexInto and canMoveBetweenCardinalNeighborsInto (or equivalently reviewed names) with caller-owned reusable outputs. Movement-cell output includes ok/reason/passable/walkCostMilli/cellVersion; cardinal output includes ok/reason/passable. Both reset every field deterministically for success, invalid index, non-neighbor, wall, blocked terrain, closed door and zero-walk-cost cases without per-call allocation

GridPathfinder.findPathInto uses only the new caller-owned/primitive MapGrid movement facts in its hot search. A focused probe replaces legacy isCellPassableByIndex, canMoveBetweenCardinalNeighbors and readCellByIndex with throw stubs and still resolves the exact path, proving the allocation-free path does not call legacy materializing APIs

ReservationLedger exposes acquireInto with typed caller-owned prepared-claim scratch, claim-id output and reusable result output. It validates the complete transaction and pending-claim contention first, then commits all claims atomically; every header/claim/conflict/capacity/output failure leaves ledger state, version and outputs deterministically unchanged or reset

M3AbilityCacheStore exposes queryAbilityInto with a caller-owned result lane containing the existing success/failure reason, value/threshold, condition/base versions and visited count for cache hit, rebuild, stale, invalid actor/lane and denied-threshold paths without a per-query result object

Focused allocation probes reuse the exact same scratch/output identities across success and failure loops for every new Into surface and record no per-call object, array, typed-array, closure or string construction in their hot implementations; legacy allocating wrappers are explicitly outside this hot-path proof

All existing MapGrid reads/movement methods, register/update/read/selectTopOffers, findPath, ReservationLedger.acquire and queryAbility signatures and behavior remain available as compatibility wrappers. New MapGrid reads do not mutate global/cell/chunk versions, dirty queue, snapshot bytes or canonical hash; existing snapshots, deterministic hashes, selection order, path results, atomic reservation reasons/cleanup and ability-cache semantics do not change
Every new type and Into surface is exported from the @wuming-town/sim-core package root and focused tests import the public root; no consumer may use an internal-file bypass

M1 hauling/building, M2 work/logistics and M3 ordinary-life 100000-tick hashes/invariants, capacity exhaustion, stale-version rejection, candidate caps, path node/output caps, all reservation channels and ability cache invalidation remain passing
No GameSession/autonomy implementation, sim-protocol, Worker, Web/Pixi/HUD, save/load, runtime dependency, PR-3 command, content or release surface changes
requiredChecks: corepack pnpm exec vitest run packages/sim-core/src/work-offers.test.ts packages/sim-core/src/map-grid.test.ts packages/sim-core/src/pathing.test.ts packages/sim-core/src/reservation-ledger.test.ts packages/sim-core/src/m3-health.test.ts packages/sim-core/src/owner-hot-path-surfaces.test.ts

history.details: WM-0169 is done. Scoped one additive compatibility repair for the four generic owner public surfaces blocking WM-0170; legacy wrappers/hashes remain, Spark is prohibited, and the task is ready/unclaimed.

Allocation-free GridPathfinder.findPathInto cannot be implemented within current allowedPaths: MapGrid.isCellPassableByIndex, canMoveBetweenCardinalNeighbors and readCellByIndex materialize result/cell objects per path node/neighbor, while map-grid.ts/tests are outside WM-0177 ownership. Removing only the final path/result allocation would leave WM-0170 blocked. Revise WM-0177 or add a strict predecessor for additive caller-owned/primitive MapGrid path facts. Evidence: coordination/reports/WM-0177-plan.md and coordination/reports/WM-0177.md.

Approved one cohesive additive WM-0177 expansion for exact map-grid.ts/map-grid.test.ts caller-owned movement facts. Product/test/export ownership is capped at 12 files; legacy MapGrid APIs, versions, dirty state, snapshots and hashes remain unchanged. WM-0177 stays blocked/claimed until the original simulation-engineer follows the exact resume procedure.
Architect approved additive MapGrid caller-owned scope in 4175afd; exact 12-file cap and original thresholds preserved.

Required full bench remains failing and cannot be waived on checkpoint 054d501. Owner-local suites pass, but the aggregate gate fails six suites and requires same-host clean-main attribution before resume.

Same-host 9-sample/2-warmup evidence proves a mixed blocker: clean main fails entity-store, M4 and map-dirty, while WM-0177 adds M3, M5 and spatial-index regressions and worsens M4/map-dirty. WM-0178 is the mandatory QA predecessor; no waiver, baseline change, threshold reduction or product edit is authorized.

saveLoadStance: Additive runtime API compatibility only. Existing snapshots remain byte/semantic compatible and no GameSession save/load format, codec, migration or public promise changes.
forbiddenPaths: packages/sim-core/src/game-session* or any WM-0170 autonomy implementation
changing MapGrid mutation, version increment, dirty queue, snapshot, restore or canonical hash semantics; exposing/casting private MapGrid lanes instead of using reviewed public Into methods
removing, narrowing or behavior-changing legacy allocating wrappers; changing existing snapshots, hashes, stable order, caps, reservation atomicity/reasons or ability semantics
allocating objects, arrays, typed arrays, closures or constructed strings inside the new Into hot paths
packages/sim-protocol/**, packages/sim-worker/**, apps/**, packages/renderer-pixi/**, packages/ui-react/** or Electron/platform files
save/load format or compatibility, new runtime dependency, PR-3 command/building/priority, content expansion or release action

modifying more than the 12 exact product/test/export files in writeOwnership; requiring a thirteenth file, a non-additive consumer change or another missing public owner surface must stop and block for a separately reviewed split
claiming or unblocking WM-0170 before WM-0177 is independently verified, integrated and done
executionLane: simulation-engineer only; Spark is prohibited because the task changes generic deterministic owner APIs, allocation behavior and reservation atomicity
resumeProcedure: WM-0178 must be independently verified, integrated and done on main, and its unchanged-workload, unchanged-baseline exact corepack pnpm bench gate must pass. If WM-0178 identifies exact sim/product files and systems architecture conditionally creates WM-0179, WM-0179 must also be independently verified, integrated and done before this procedure continues.

Under project-director control, refresh the existing WM-0177 branch from the repaired main lineage and preserve reviewed checkpoint 054d501 through an explicit conflict/diff audit. Do not add a linked worktree, lower the 12-file product cap or import unrelated performance repairs into WM-0177.

Before unblock, the original simulation-engineer must rerun the complete public API audit from coordination/reports/WM-0177-plan.md and same-host isolated paired benchmark probes. M3, M5 and spatial-index task deltas plus the additional M4/map-dirty worsening must be explained and eliminated; owner-local passes alone are insufficient.

Only after those gates pass may the original simulation-engineer run: node .agents/skills/wuming-town-agent-workflow/scripts/taskctl.mjs unblock --id WM-0177 --agent simulation-engineer --summary "WM-0178 canonical benchmark gate repair is verified, integrated and done; repaired main passes the fixed-baseline full bench, paired WM-0177 attribution is reviewed, and the original acceptance resumes without waiver."

Resume only the original max-12-file owner-surface scope, rerun every existing required check, and require corepack pnpm bench to exit zero against the unchanged baseline. Any remaining task delta, full-suite failure, thirteenth file, hidden allocation or semantic change blocks again instead of using a waiver.
singleWriterRule: WM-0178 is ready and unclaimed and becomes the sole QA harness/evidence writer when claimed. WM-0177 remains blocked and claimed by its original simulation-engineer with no concurrent product edits until the exact resume procedure is satisfied. WM-0170 remains blocked behind WM-0177 with unchanged thresholds; WM-0171..WM-0175 retain their existing dependencies.

blocker.reason: The required fixed-baseline full benchmark has a reviewed mixed failure and no waiver is permitted. Same-host clean main 4175afd fails entity-store (+52.62%), M4 (+26.52%) and map-dirty (+25.17%); checkpoint 054d501 additionally regresses M3 (+44.79% versus main), M5 (+38.30%) and spatial-index (+24.19%) and worsens M4 (+13.24%) and map-dirty (+19.82%). Owner-local passes do not satisfy the aggregate gate. WM-0178 must repair and independently verify the canonical main gate before WM-0177 can eliminate its task-associated deltas and resume. Evidence: checkpoint 054d501 and coordination/reports/WM-0178-plan.md.
resumeProcedure: WM-0178 must be independently verified, integrated and done on main, and its unchanged-workload, unchanged-baseline exact corepack pnpm bench gate must pass. If WM-0178 identifies exact sim/product files and systems architecture conditionally creates WM-0179, WM-0179 must also be independently verified, integrated and done before this procedure continues.

Under project-director control, refresh the existing WM-0177 branch from the repaired main lineage and preserve reviewed checkpoint 054d501 through an explicit conflict/diff audit. Do not add a linked worktree, lower the 12-file product cap or import unrelated performance repairs into WM-0177.

Before unblock, the original simulation-engineer must rerun the complete public API audit from coordination/reports/WM-0177-plan.md and same-host isolated paired benchmark probes. M3, M5 and spatial-index task deltas plus the additional M4/map-dirty worsening must be explained and eliminated; owner-local passes alone are insufficient.

Only after those gates pass may the original simulation-engineer run: node .agents/skills/wuming-town-agent-workflow/scripts/taskctl.mjs unblock --id WM-0177 --agent simulation-engineer --summary "WM-0178 canonical benchmark gate repair is verified, integrated and done; repaired main passes the fixed-baseline full bench, paired WM-0177 attribution is reviewed, and the original acceptance resumes without waiver."

Resume only the original max-12-file owner-surface scope, rerun every existing required check, and require corepack pnpm bench to exit zero against the unchanged baseline. Any remaining task delta, full-suite failure, thirteenth file, hidden allocation or semantic change blocks again instead of using a waiver.
